title: Free Audio Production Workflows
description: Open, local-first workflows for common music, podcast, repair, mastering, and sound-design problems.

entries.title: Podcast Dialogue Clarity
symptoms: listener turns volume up but words stay unclear
causes: too much low-mid buildup between 150 Hz and 350 Hz
not enough controlled presence between 2 kHz and 5 kHz
background noise competes with consonants
fixes: high-pass below the voice fundamental, often 70 Hz to 100 Hz for speech
cut 180 Hz to 320 Hz gently before boosting presence
use light compression with makeup gain, then de-ess only if sibilance appears

entries.title: Room Reverb And Echo Control
symptoms: voice has a boxy room sound
causes: strong early reflections from walls or desk surfaces
long room decay compared with speech gaps
microphone is too far from the source
fixes: move the mic closer and lower input gain before using software repair
use a downward expander for pauses and a narrow low-mid cut for boxiness
avoid heavy reverb removal when it creates metallic artifacts

entries.title: Sibilance De-Essing
causes: narrow energy spike between 5 kHz and 9 kHz
bright microphone or aggressive presence boost
compression raises consonants too much
fixes: find the sibilant band with a narrow EQ sweep, then use a de-esser or dynamic EQ
de-ess before final brightening if the compressor exaggerates s sounds
reduce only the harsh moments rather than cutting all top end

entries.title: Phase And Mono Compatibility
symptoms: mix sounds wide in stereo but weak in mono
causes: wide low frequencies with opposite polarity between channels
stereo widening applied to mono-critical instruments
multi-mic tracks are not time aligned
fixes: sum low frequencies below 120 Hz to mono
check polarity and small timing offsets on multi-mic sources
keep lead vocal, kick, bass, and snare stable near the center

entries.title: Stereo Width Translation
symptoms: mix feels impressive on headphones but small on speakers
wide elements distract from the vocal
causes: side signal is louder than the center in important bands
too many instruments occupy the same wide image
low mids are widened and blur the groove
fixes: use width mostly on supporting textures, delays, pads, and doubles
keep low-end and core rhythm more centered
automate width wider in choruses instead of widening the whole song

entries.title: Arrangement Energy Build
symptoms: drop does not feel bigger than verse
causes: too many high-energy elements are present before the chorus
no contrast in register, density, or rhythm
automation stays static across sections
fixes: remove one or two parts before the chorus so the hook has contrast
add riser, reverse reverb, drum fill, or filtered noise before the transition
automate level, width, brightness, or reverb send into the high-energy section

entries.title: Cinematic Impact Sound Design
symptoms: hit does not cut through the scene
causes: impact has only one layer
transient and tail are not separated
low layer is masking the mid punch
fixes: layer a short transient click, mid punch, sub drop, and textured tail
high-pass noisy texture layers so the sub remains clean
compress or limit the bus after balancing layers

entries.title: Noisy Live Recording Repair
symptoms: live recording has hiss or crowd noise
performance is useful but technically rough
noise reduction makes the track watery
causes: constant broadband noise below the music
automatic repair is too aggressive
room reflections and audience noise overlap the source
fixes: use gentle denoise and accept a small amount of natural noise
repair clicks and hum separately before broad noise reduction
use EQ to reduce masking bands instead of trying to remove every artifact

entries.title: Streaming Mastering Loudness
symptoms: streaming playback sounds quieter than expected
causes: too much limiter gain reduction
low-end peaks trigger the limiter
loudness target ignores genre and platform context
fixes: control sub peaks before the final limiter
aim for a balanced integrated loudness and keep true peak headroom
compare against a loudness-matched reference instead of chasing maximum level

entries.title: Semantic Sample Search By Timbre
symptoms: sample library search by filename is slow
snare is the right genre but wrong texture
impact sample has the wrong energy
causes: filenames do not describe brightness, decay, punch, or mood
tempo and key metadata miss timbre
sample choice is made before the mix context is known
fixes: tag samples by brightness, decay, punch, noise, tone, and energy
rank results by requested mood and measured spectral features
preview samples loudness-matched to avoid choosing only the loudest file
